prose:
Single‐point electronic energy of the water molecule

Method provenance
• Property: Total electronic energy (electrons + nuclei), −137.98 eV
• Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical extended-tight-binding method; no Gaussian basis)
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/runs/water_sp_xtb/water_xtb.xyz; single-point evaluation (no re-optimization) → notation: GFN2-xTB//input xyz
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet)
• Software/backend: xtb through chemkit single-point-energy skill (see cli_invocation in JSON; xtb backend version as shipped with chemkit). Density-fitting inherent to GFN2-xTB.
• Convergence & caveats: single-point calculation completed; integrity check status "ok", trustworthy: true; no warnings reported. Energy zero is xtb’s isolated-atoms reference, so absolute values are not directly comparable to ab-initio energies.

Result
Total electronic energy (GFN2-xTB, gas phase):
Eₑ = −137.98 eV

Reproducibility
Command executed (from result JSON):
  /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py sp --method xtb /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/runs/water_sp_xtb/water_xtb.xyz --out <output>.json
A live engine log was written during execution (see the .out file in the current working directory) alongside the JSON result (water_xtb_sp.json).
